definition: A named message chain — a logical grouping of related **messages** sharing a common origin. [Flows are organized in *folders*](/dashboard/flows#organizing-flows-in-folders) and defined by a name, optional mappings, and business properties.

A named message chain — a logical grouping of related **flow traces** sharing a common origin. [Flows are organized in *folders*](/dashboard/flows#organizing-flows-in-folders) and defined by a name, optional mappings, and business properties.

A single tracked message instance within a **flow**. It records the full lifecycle of one execution: status, timestamps, properties, and the chain of processing steps.

Each flow trace can be searched and inspected in the Dashboard.

A single tracked message instance within a **flow**. It records the full lifecycle of one execution: status, timestamps, properties, and the chain of processing steps.

Each flow trace can be searched and inspected in the Dashboard, and is loaded via **flow trace importing**.

The process of ingesting **flow trace** data into the Dashboard — automatically via Logic Apps or Function Apps, or manually. Once imported, messages become visible and searchable in the flow monitoring view.
A set of components that supports commonly used integration patterns in Azure Logic App workflows. [Learn more on how to install the Invictus Framework](/framework/installation).

An on-premise solution that reduces the need for in-depth Azure knowledge and allows business users to focus on the status of their integrations. [Learn more on how to install the Invictus Dashboard](/dashboard/installation).

Stands for **Domain**, **Service**, **Action**, and **Version** — the four business properties Invictus uses to categorize messages. In the Dashboard search you can filter by any combination of these values to find all messages of the same integration type.

An identifier that groups all **flow trace** instances belonging to the same end-to-end transaction. Filtering by Chain ID in the Dashboard shows every message processed as part of that single transaction, even across multiple flows.

The state of a **flow trace** as shown in the Dashboard. There are four possible states:

- **Active** — the message is currently being processed.
- **Completed** — the message finished successfully.
- **Error** — the message was suspended, dehydrated, or terminated with a failure.
- **Ignored** — an operator manually dismissed the message (typically after reviewing a failure).

A Dashboard action that re-triggers a **flow trace** from the beginning. Resubmit restarts the first workflow in the chain, even if the original message completed successfully.

See also **Resume**, which only re-runs the failed steps rather than the whole chain.

A Dashboard action that re-runs only the failed steps of a **flow trace**, without restarting the entire chain from the beginning. Resume is only available for messages in **Error** status.

See also **Resubmit**, which always restarts from the first workflow.
A container used to organize **flows** in the Dashboard sidebar. Folders can be nested into a hierarchy with sub-folders, giving teams a clear overview of their integration landscape.

Stands for **Domain**, **Service**, **Action**, and **Version** — four special **tracked properties** that Invictus reads during **flow trace importing** to map workflow runs to the correct Dashboard flow.

These values must match the corresponding properties configured on the flow in the Dashboard. Invictus also reads a `WorkflowName` field alongside DSAV to resolve the exact flow mapping.

The Invictus name for the Azure Logic Apps `clientTrackingId`. It groups all **flow traces** belonging to the same transaction.

Because async flows may regenerate this ID during routing, developers must reset it as early as possible in each workflow to keep the chain intact.

Custom metadata fields set on Azure Logic App actions that Invictus reads when importing **flow traces**. They drive the key Dashboard features:

- **DSAV** and `WorkflowName` — map workflow runs to the correct flow.
- `x-iv-parent-workflow-run-id` — links child workflows to their parent to build the **execution tree**.
- `Milestone` / `EventText` — annotate processing steps shown in the flow detail view.
- `x-iv-messagecontent-*` — expose input/output content of specific actions in the Dashboard.

The hierarchical view of parent and child Azure Logic App workflow runs linked within a single **flow trace**. Invictus builds this tree from the `x-iv-parent-workflow-run-id` **tracked property** set by developers when chaining workflows.

The execution tree is visible in the Dashboard flow detail view and shows the full processing chain for a message.

An **Invictus Framework** component that resolves the actual error from a failed Azure Logic App workflow run. It exposes a `/api/ResolveException` endpoint that retrieves the underlying error message — replacing the generic Azure error — and can optionally translate it to human-readable text via a configurable regex translation table.

An **Invictus Framework** component that enables publish/subscribe messaging between Azure Logic App workflows using Azure Service Bus as a message broker. Workflows interact with it via three HTTP endpoints: Publish, Subscribe, and Acknowledge.

For messages exceeding the configured size limit, PubSub automatically applies the **claim-check pattern** — offloading the content to Azure Blob Storage and restoring it on Subscribe.

An **Invictus Framework** component that ensures Azure Logic App workflows execute in a defined order without relying on concurrency throttling. Workflows take a numbered position in a named sequence, wait their turn, perform their work, then signal completion so the next workflow can proceed.

This allows parallel-triggered workflows to process messages in order without blocking the entire Logic App.

An **Invictus Framework** component (originating from a BizTalk pattern) that enriches integration messages during processing. It can:

- **Promote** values from an SQL database into the workflow context, using the message content or context to build the query.
- **Transform** XML content using an XSLT stylesheet stored in Azure Blob Storage.

An **Invictus Framework** behavior implemented in **PubSub**: when a published message body exceeds the configured size limit (default 20,000 bytes), the content is automatically stored in Azure Blob Storage and the message is sent with an empty body.

The Subscribe action transparently retrieves the content from storage, so consumers receive the full payload without needing to handle the offload themselves.

*See also the [claim-check integration pattern](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/patterns/claim-check).*
